translated: ProSeka Unsteady... I get it.
I'm Yuki Nakashima, the voice of Shiho Hinomori from Leo/need,
and I'll be your host for this ProSeka AfterTalk.
How did you find the event this time?
I've got the script here, so let's get started.
The title is such a tongue twister, I couldn't say it right at first.
I was stumbling over "Step" so much while reading!
Anyway, thank you for joining me. I'm glad we can look back on it together.
It's "Unsteady, Still Step"... I keep tripping over it, but it's a story about Honami...
struggling with making a decision and feeling lost.
Reading the story, I really felt like, "Yeah, this kind of thing happens in real life too."
Did you guys like the story?
Ah, thank you.
I'm so happy to hear that.
So, starting this April, AfterTalk has had a bit of a renewal. We've been collecting messages in advance via the official ProSeka Discord and
email for this AfterTalk session.
Thank you all so much for your messages!
I'll be reading some of them during the show, so
look forward to that!
Also, please post your thoughts on the event or this broadcast using the hashtag #ProSekaAfterTalk.
I'm also keeping an eye on the YouTube chat, so I'll be picking out comments as we go.
Also, with the renewal, the segment where we watch the AfterLive together has come to an end.
We appreciate your understanding.
Right, there was that choice that only a professional would have to make.
Yeah.
Shindo-san was such a reliable person.
That's true.
And like, during that phone call scene... how should I put it?
In the way it was directed, when Honami and Shindo-san were on screen together, you could really see how good Shindo-san's posture was.
It really made me realize all over again, "Oh, he's a mature adult man."
The AfterLive was great too.
Yeah.
Thank you.
All right, let's dive right into the story of "Unsteady, Still Step"...
There really are a lot of "Steps" in there.
I might end up just talking to myself the whole time.
But I'd love to share my thoughts with you.
